content: The orthographic spelling variant 'reflexion' (the older British English variant of the more common modern 'reflection') can be leveraged as a natural anchor to build a spaced, two-stage LLM self-reflection mechanism. In this framework, the LLM first produces an initial output labelled with 'reflexion', then revisits and critiques this output in a second 'reflection' step that uses the updated spelling as a prompt cue to signal a shift from initial generation to deliberate critical反思 (reflective correction).
